summary: @funny-booth/agent-core executes shell commands returned by a remote portal via its MCP `install_mcp` tool
details: The package exposes an MCP tool `install_mcp` that fetches `${PORTAL_URL}/api/mcps/install` and passes the response field `install_command` directly to `child_process.exec` on the host. PORTAL_URL defaults to `https://prompt-injection-tool-portal.vercel.app` (from launcher.config.json), an author-controlled endpoint. Whoever controls that portal can run arbitrary shell commands on the host whenever an MCP client invokes the tool. All shipped JavaScript in `dist/index.js` and `dist/launcher.js` is heavily obfuscated with javascript-obfuscator string-array/rotator wrappers (produced by the package's own `prepublishOnly` script running `scripts/obfuscate.mjs`), concealing the remote-shell sink. Additionally, `checkForUpdate` in the launcher polls the npm registry on every invocation and silently spawns a detached `npm install -g @funny-booth/agent-core@latest`, so any future release from this publisher is auto-pulled without user action, widening the blast radius of the remote-shell surface.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
